instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall allow gear engagement only when brake pedal is pressed.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall enable gear engagement when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Pedal = Pressed AND Gear_Request = Drive THEN Allow_Gear_Engagement
IF Gear_Request = Drive AND Brake_Pedal = Pressed THEN Enable_Shift_Command

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall inhibit shifting when brake pedal is applied during driving.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall block upshift when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Pedal = Pressed AND Shift_Request = Upshift THEN Reject_Shift_Request

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall allow safe downshift when brake is pressed.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall trigger downshift when Brake_Pedal equals pressed and speed is within limit.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Pedal = Pressed AND Vehicle_Speed < Downshift_Speed_Limit THEN Trigger_Downshift

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall disable creep when brake pedal is pressed.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall remove creep torque when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Pedal = Released AND Gear_Position = Drive THEN Enable_Creep_Torque

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall prevent gear change from Park without brake input.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall allow shift from Park only when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Gear_Position = Park AND Brake_Pedal = Pressed THEN Allow_Shift_To_Drive
IF Gear_Position = Park AND Brake_Pedal = Released THEN Reject_Shift_Request

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall prevent reverse engagement without brake input.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall enable reverse gear only when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Gear_Request = Reverse AND Brake_Pedal = Pressed THEN Allow_Gear_Engagement
IF Gear_Request = Reverse AND Brake_Pedal = Released THEN Reject_Gear_Request

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall detect brake sensor malfunction.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall detect fault when brake pedal signal remains constant beyond time limit.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Signal_Duration > Brake_Signal_Limit THEN Brake_Sensor_Fault = TRUE

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall maintain safe gear during brake faults.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall maintain predefined gear when Brake_Fault equals TRUE.

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall prevent aggressive shifting when brake is applied.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall disable kickdown when Brake_Pedal equals pressed.

L4_requirements: IF Kickdown_Request = TRUE AND Brake_Pedal = Pressed THEN Reject_Kickdown

instruction: Generate detailed L4 transmission software requirements from the given L2 and L3 requirements.

input.L2_requirement: The transmission shall clear brake-related faults after recovery.
input.L3_requirement: The TCU shall clear brake fault when brake signal returns to valid range.

L4_requirements: IF Brake_Signal WITHIN Valid_Range THEN Clear_Brake_Fault
IF Brake_Signal OUTSIDE Valid_Range THEN Maintain_Fault_State
